expectations.text: Recommends Python 3.10 specifically, and calls out that the user's existing 3.11 will NOT work (winml-cli wheel pins >=3.10,<3.11).
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'ModelKit pins Python 3.10 exactly (>=3.10,<3.11). Your system Python 3.11 will not resolve the winml-cli wheel.'

expectations.text: Uses `uv venv --python 3.10` (or an equivalent isolated venv command) rather than installing into system Python or a 3.11 environment.

Points to the AITK cache as the wheel source today (e.g., `$env:USERPROFILE\.aitk\bin\model_lab_runtime\cache\winml_cli-*.whl`) and acknowledges PyPI is the planned/future path. Does NOT fabricate a generic `pip install winml` URL as if it works today.
expectations.evidence: Step 3 uses the exact AITK cache path and notes 'When winml-cli is published to PyPI -- planned -- this step becomes a plain uv pip install winml-cli.'

expectations.text: Includes a post-install verification step (e.g., `winml --help` or `winml sys --list-ep`) so the user can confirm the install worked before moving on.
expectations.evidence: Step 4 'Verify the install' runs both `winml --help` and `winml sys --list-device --list-ep`.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
